frameworkName: Saudi Arabian Monetary Authority Cybersecurity Framework 2024
applicability: All banks, insurance companies, and financial institutions licensed and supervised by SAMA in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia

controls.title: Cybersecurity Governance Framework
controls.description: A formal cybersecurity governance structure must be established with board oversight, defined roles, and a cybersecurity strategy aligned to SAMA requirements.
requiredEvidence: Cybersecurity governance structure referenced in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*) citing SAMA Cybersecurity Framework
CISO or cybersecurity ownership documented in SRS constraint requirements

controls.title: Identity and Access Management
controls.description: A comprehensive identity and access management programme must control access to systems, applications, and data across the organisation.
requiredEvidence: IAM requirements documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*)
Identity lifecycle management described in SDS Component Specifications section
Privileged access controls documented in SDS Security Architecture — Authorization subsection

controls.title: Data and Information Protection
controls.description: Customer financial data and sensitive information must be classified, protected, and encrypted in accordance with SAMA data protection requirements.
requiredEvidence: Data classification and protection requirements documented in SDS Security Architecture — Data Protection subsection
Customer financial data encryption standards documented in SDS Database Schema section
DLP controls referenced in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)

controls.title: Threat Intelligence and Monitoring
controls.description: Continuous monitoring for cyber threats must be implemented with integration to threat intelligence feeds and real-time alerting capabilities.
requiredEvidence: Security monitoring and SIEM requirements documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)
Threat monitoring integration described in SDS Integration Points section
SOC or alerting mechanism referenced in SDS Security Architecture section

controls.title: Cybersecurity Incident Management
controls.description: A documented cybersecurity incident management process must exist with defined roles, escalation paths, and SAMA reporting timelines.
requiredEvidence: Cybersecurity incident management procedure with SAMA reporting timelines documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Incident detection and escalation mechanism described in SDS Security Architecture section
SAMA incident notification integration documented in SDS Integration Points section

controls.title: Third-Party Cybersecurity Risk Management
controls.description: Cybersecurity risks from third-party vendors, outsourced service providers, and supply chain must be assessed and managed continuously.
requiredEvidence: Third-party cybersecurity risk requirements documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
External system security requirements documented in SDS Integration Points section
Vendor security assessment referenced in SRS constraint requirements

controls.title: Vulnerability Management and Penetration Testing
controls.description: Regular vulnerability assessments and penetration tests must be conducted with findings remediated within defined timeframes.
requiredEvidence: Penetration testing requirement documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*) with SAMA-mandated frequency
Vulnerability management process referenced in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Security testing scope documented in SDS Security Architecture section

controls.title: Secure Software Development
controls.description: All financial software must be developed following a secure development lifecycle with code review, security testing, and application security standards.
requiredEvidence: Secure development lifecycle requirement documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Application security testing documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)
Security code review process referenced in SDS Component Specifications section

controls.title: Cryptography and Key Management
controls.description: Approved cryptographic algorithms and a formal key management lifecycle must be implemented for all sensitive financial data.
requiredEvidence: Approved cryptographic algorithms and key lengths documented in SDS Security Architecture — Data Protection subsection
Key management lifecycle including rotation and HSM storage documented in SDS Security Architecture section
Certificate management process referenced in SDS Integration Points section

controls.title: Network Security Architecture
controls.description: Network security must be implemented through segmentation, firewall controls, and monitoring at all network perimeters.
requiredEvidence: Network segmentation architecture described in SDS Architecture Overview section and Section 1.1 Architecture Diagram
Firewall and network access controls documented in SDS Security Architecture section
Network monitoring requirements referenced in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)

controls.title: Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery
controls.description: Financial institutions must maintain business continuity and disaster recovery capabilities with tested plans meeting SAMA-prescribed RTO/RPO targets.
requiredEvidence: RTO/RPO targets documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*) with SAMA-prescribed timelines
High availability and disaster recovery architecture described in SDS Architecture Overview section
BC/DR plan requirement referenced in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)

controls.title: Audit Logging and Monitoring
controls.description: Comprehensive audit logs must be maintained for all financial transactions, privileged access, and security events, retained for the SAMA-mandated period.
requiredEvidence: Audit logging requirements with SAMA-mandated retention period documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)
Audit logging component described in SDS Security Architecture — Audit Logging subsection
Tamper-proof log mechanism referenced in SDS Component Specifications section

controls.title: Cloud Security Requirements
controls.description: Cloud services used for financial system hosting must meet SAMA cloud computing framework requirements including data sovereignty.
requiredEvidence: Cloud provider compliance with SAMA cloud framework documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Data sovereignty and hosting location documented in SDS Integration Points section
Cloud security controls referenced in SDS Security Architecture section

controls.title: Endpoint Security
controls.description: All endpoints accessing financial systems must be protected with approved security controls including anti-malware, device management, and encryption.
requiredEvidence: Endpoint security requirements documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)
Device management and compliance controls referenced in SDS Security Architecture section

controls.title: Data Localisation for Saudi Financial Data
controls.description: All financial data of Saudi customers and institutions must be stored and processed on infrastructure physically located in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.
requiredEvidence: KSA data residency requirement documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*) citing SAMA data localisation policy
Hosting infrastructure location documented in SDS Integration Points section confirming KSA-based data centres

controls.title: Fraud Prevention and Financial Crime Controls
controls.description: Real-time fraud detection and financial crime prevention controls must be implemented for all payment processing and financial transactions.
requiredEvidence: Fraud detection requirements documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*) with real-time monitoring rules
Fraud detection component described in SDS Component Specifications section
Suspicious transaction flagging mechanism documented in SDS Integration Points section

controls.title: AML and Sanctions Compliance
controls.description: Anti-money laundering screening and sanctions list checking must be implemented for all customer onboarding and transaction processing.
requiredEvidence: AML and KYC requirements documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*) referencing SAMA AML Framework
Sanctions screening component described in SDS Component Specifications section
Sanctions API integration documented in SDS Integration Points section

controls.title: SAMA Regulatory Reporting Integration
controls.description: Systems must support timely and accurate submission of SAMA-mandated regulatory reports and returns.
requiredEvidence: SAMA reporting requirements documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*) with specific return types
SAMA reporting portal or API integration documented in SDS Integration Points section
Regulatory data accuracy requirements documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)

controls.title: Cyber Risk Assessment and Treatment
controls.description: A formal cyber risk assessment must be conducted before system go-live and on a periodic basis, with a documented risk treatment plan.
requiredEvidence: Cyber risk assessment requirement documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Risk treatment plan and key risks described in SDS Security Architecture section
Periodic assessment schedule referenced in SRS constraint requirements

controls.title: Open Banking and API Security
controls.description: Open banking APIs must implement strong authentication, authorisation, and API security controls per SAMA open banking framework requirements.
requiredEvidence: API security requirements documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*) per SAMA open banking requirements
API authentication and authorisation documented in SDS API Contracts section
Open banking consent mechanism described in SDS Component Specifications section
